text: Planning a trip is often as exciting as the journey itself. When I visited Japan, I spent weeks researching flights and accommodations. The new culture amazed me—temples, etiquette, and customs felt both foreign and fascinating. Trying local food was a highlight, from steaming ramen to delicate sushi. However, language barriers made simple tasks challenging, like ordering a meal or asking for directions. Despite the confusion, these moments became cherished memories. Now, I look back with a smile, knowing that the small struggles enriched my understanding of the world.
